title: hibbertia hortiorum
intro: Hibbertia hortiorum, also known as the Guinea flower, is a small, shrub-like plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, coughs, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Hibbertia hortiorum include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
